name: ObjectAttributes
summary: Attributes (color, material, layer,...) associated with a rhino object

constructors.name: ObjectAttributes()
constructors.summary: Initializes a new instance of the ObjectAttributes class

constructors.name: ObjectAttributes(SerializationInfo, StreamingContext)
constructors.summary: Initializes a new instance of the ObjectAttributes class

methods.name: AddHideInDetailOverride
methods.summary: Make this object hidden in a given detail

methods.name: AddToGroup
methods.summary: Adds object to the group with specified index by appending index to group list. If the object is already in group, nothing is changed.

params.name: groupIndex
params.doc: The index that will be added.

methods.name: ClearRenderingAttributes
methods.summary: Clears, or resets the rendering attributes.

methods.name: ComputedPlotColor(RhinoDoc, Guid)
methods.signature: public Color ComputedPlotColor(RhinoDoc document, Guid viewportId)

methods.name: ComputedPlotWeight(RhinoDoc, Guid)
methods.signature: public double ComputedPlotWeight(RhinoDoc document, Guid viewportId)

methods.name: ConstructConstObject
methods.signature: protected void ConstructConstObject(Object parentObject, int subobjectIndex)
methods.summary: Assigns a parent object and a sub-object index to this.

methods.name: Dispose()
methods.summary: Actively reclaims unmanaged resources that this instance uses.

methods.name: Dispose(Boolean)
methods.summary: For derived class implementers. This method is called with argument true when class user calls Dispose(), while with argument false when the Garbage Collector invokes the finalizer, or Finalize() method.You must reclaim all used unmanaged resources in both cases, and can use this chance to call Dispose on disposable fields if the argument is true.Also, you must call the base virtual method within your overriding method.

params.name: disposing
params.doc: true if the call comes from the Dispose() method; false if it comes from the Garbage Collector finalizer.

methods.name: DrawColor(RhinoDoc)
methods.summary: Gets an object's draw color, which is based on the object's color source

methods.name: DrawColor(RhinoDoc, Guid)
methods.signature: public Color DrawColor(RhinoDoc document, Guid viewportId)
methods.summary: Gets an object's draw color, which is based on the object's color source

methods.name: Duplicate
methods.summary: Constructs a copy of this ObjectAttributes instance.

returns.doc: A new instance on success, or null on failure.

methods.name: EnsurePrivateCopy
methods.summary: If you want to keep a copy of this class around by holding onto it in a variable after a command completes, call EnsurePrivateCopy to make sure that this class is not tied to the document. You can call this function as many times as you want.

methods.name: Finalize
methods.summary: Passively reclaims unmanaged resources when the class user did not explicitly call Dispose().

methods.name: GetCustomLinetype
methods.summary: Get an optional custom linetype associated with these attributes. If null, then the attributes use the linetype index to determine it's linetype

methods.name: GetCustomSectionStyle
methods.summary: Get an optional custom section style associated with these attributes.

methods.name: GetDisplayModeOverride
methods.summary: Returns the id of the display mode of an objects. Object display modes are view based. Thus, it is possible to have an object display different in different views.

returns.doc: A display node id if the object has a display mode override for the viewport; otherwise Guid.Empty is returned.

methods.name: GetGroupList
methods.summary: Returns an array of GroupCount group indices. If GroupCount is zero, then GetGroupList() returns null.

returns.doc: An array of group indices. null might be returned in place of an empty array.

methods.name: GetHideInDetailOverrides
methods.summary: Get list of details that this object is supposed to be hidden in

methods.name: GetObjectData
methods.signature: public virtual void GetObjectData(SerializationInfo info, StreamingContext context)
methods.summary: Populates a System.Runtime.Serialization.SerializationInfo with the data needed to serialize the target object.

methods.name: GetUserString
methods.summary: Gets a user string.

params.name: key
params.doc: id used to retrieve the string.

returns.doc: string associated with the key if successful. null if no key was found.

methods.name: GetUserStrings
methods.summary: Gets an independent copy of the collection of (user text key, user text value) pairs attached to this object.

returns.doc: A collection of key strings and values strings. This

methods.name: HasDisplayModeOverride
methods.summary: Determines if an object has a display mode override for a given viewport.

returns.doc: true if the object has a display mode override for the viewport; otherwise, false.

methods.name: HasHideInDetailOverrideSet
methods.summary: Is this object supposed to be hidden in a given detail

methods.name: IsInGroup
methods.summary: Determines if an object belong to a group or not.

params.name: groupIndex
params.doc: The index that will be tested.

methods.name: IsValidWithLog
methods.summary: Determines if an object is valid. Also provides a report on errors if this object happens not to be valid.

params.name: log
params.doc: A textual log. This out parameter is assigned during this call.

returns.doc: true if this object is valid; false otherwise.

methods.name: NonConstOperation
methods.summary: For derived classes implementers. Defines the necessary implementation to free the instance from being constant.

methods.name: OnSwitchToNonConst
methods.summary: Is called when a non-constant operation first occurs.

methods.name: RemoveDisplayModeOverride()
methods.summary: By default, objects are drawn using the display mode of the viewport that the object is being drawn in. Setting a specific display mode, instructs Rhino to always use that display mode, regardless of the viewport's mode. This function resets an object to use the viewport's display mode for all viewports.

methods.name: RemoveDisplayModeOverride(Guid)
methods.summary: By default, objects are drawn using the display mode of the viewport that the object is being drawn in. Setting a specific display mode, instructs Rhino to always use that display mode, regardless of the viewport's mode. This function resets an object to use the viewport's display mode.

params.name: rhinoViewportId
params.doc: viewport that display mode overrides should be cleared from.

methods.name: RemoveFromAllGroups
methods.summary: Removes object from all groups.

methods.name: RemoveFromGroup
methods.summary: removes object from the group with specified index. If the object is not in the group, nothing is changed.

params.name: groupIndex
params.doc: The index that will be removed.

methods.name: RemoveHideInDetailOverride
methods.summary: Remove hidden in detail flag for a specific detail

methods.name: SetDisplayModeOverride(DisplayModeDescription)
methods.summary: By default, objects are drawn using the display mode of the viewport that the object is being drawn in. Setting a specific display mode, instructs Rhino to always use that display mode, regardless of the viewport's mode. This version affects the object's display mode for all viewports.

methods.name: SetDisplayModeOverride(DisplayModeDescription, Guid)
methods.summary: By default, objects are drawn using the display mode of the viewport that the object is being drawn in. Setting a specific display mode, instructs Rhino to always use that display mode, regardless of the viewport's mode. This version sets a display mode for a specific viewport.

methods.name: SetUserString
methods.signature: public bool SetUserString(string key, string value)
methods.summary: Attach a user string (key,value combination) to this geometry.

params.name: key
params.doc: id used to retrieve this string.

params.name: value
params.doc: string associated with key. If null, the key will be removed

methods.name: ToJSON
methods.summary: Create a JSON string representation of this object

methods.name: Transform
methods.summary: Apply a transformation.

properties.name: CastsShadows
properties.summary: Gets or sets an object's casts shadows property, or whether or not an object casts shadows on other objects and a ground plane.

properties.name: ClippingPlaneLabelStyle
properties.summary: Defines how a label for a clipping plane object should be shown

properties.name: ColorSource
properties.summary: The color used to display an object is specified in one of three ways. If ColorSource is ON::color_from_layer, then the object's layer ON_Layer::Color() is used. If ColorSource is ON::color_from_object, then value of m_color is used. If ColorSource is ON::color_from_material, then the diffuse color of the object's render material is used. See ON_3dmObjectAttributes::MaterialSource() to determine where to get the definition of the object's render material.

properties.name: CustomMeshingParameters
properties.summary: Returns or sets the per-object render meshing parameters, which controls the object's render mesh density. If this property is null, then the object uses the document's render meshing parameters. To remove the per-object render meshing parameters, set this property to null.

properties.name: Decals
properties.summary: Gets all object decals associated with this object.

properties.name: DisplayOrder
properties.summary: Display order used to force objects to be drawn on top or behind each other. Larger numbers draw on top of smaller numbers. 0 = draw object in standard depth buffered order<0 = draw object behind "normal" draw order objects>0 = draw object on top of "normal" draw order objects

properties.name: Disposed
properties.summary: Indicates if this object has been disposed or the document it originally belonged to has been disposed.

properties.name: File3dmMeshModifiers
properties.summary: Get an object that provides access to mesh modifiers when the attributes is for a File3dmObject.

properties.name: GroupCount
properties.summary: number of groups object belongs to.

properties.name: HasMapping
properties.summary: A mapping from any plug-in source is associated with these attributes Need to do this here to respond correctly to ModifyObjectAttributes event

properties.name: HasUserData
properties.summary: Gets true if this class has any custom information attached to it through UserData.

properties.name: HatchBackgroundFillColor
properties.summary: Fill color for hatches (default is Color.Empty)

properties.name: HatchBoundaryVisible
properties.summary: Draw the bounrdaries for a hatch (default is false)

properties.name: IsDocumentControlled
properties.summary: (Overrides CommonObject.IsDocumentControlled.)

properties.name: IsInstanceDefinitionObject
properties.summary: Use this query to determine if an object is part of an instance definition.

properties.name: IsValid
properties.summary: Tests an object to see if it is valid.

properties.name: LayerIndex
properties.summary: Gets or sets an associated layer index. Layer definitions in an OpenNURBS model are stored in a layer table. The layer table is conceptually an array of ON_Layer classes. Every OpenNURBS object in a model is on some layer. The object's layer is specified by zero based indices into the ON_Layer array.

properties.name: LinetypeIndex
properties.summary: Gets or sets the linetype index. Linetype definitions in an OpenNURBS model are stored in a linetype table. The linetype table is conceptually an array of ON_Linetype classes. Every OpenNURBS object in a model references some linetype. The object's linetype is specified by zero based indices into the ON_Linetype array.Index 0 is reserved for continuous linetype (no pattern).

properties.name: LinetypePatternScale
properties.summary: Per object linetype scale

properties.name: LinetypeSource
properties.summary: The Linetype used to display an object is specified in one of two ways. If LinetypeSource is ON::linetype_from_layer, then the object's layer ON_Layer::Linetype() is used. If LinetypeSource is ON::linetype_from_object, then value of m_linetype is used.

properties.name: MaterialIndex
properties.summary: Gets or sets the material index. If you want something simple and fast, set the index of the rendering material.

properties.name: MaterialRefs
properties.summary: If you are developing a high quality plug-in renderer, and a user is assigning a custom render material to this object, then add rendering material information to the MaterialRefs dictionary. Note to developers: As soon as the MaterialRefs dictionary contains items rendering material queries slow down. Do not populate the MaterialRefs dictionary when setting the MaterialIndex will take care of your needs.

properties.name: MaterialSource
properties.summary: Determines if the simple material should come from the object or from it's layer. High quality rendering plug-ins should use m_rendering_attributes.

properties.name: Mode
properties.summary: An object must be in one of three modes: normal, locked or hidden. If an object is in normal mode, then the object's layer controls visibility and selectability. If an object is locked, then the object's layer controls visibility by the object cannot be selected. If the object is hidden, it is not visible and it cannot be selected.

properties.name: Name
properties.summary: Gets or sets an object optional text name. More than one object in a model can have the same name and some objects may have no name.

properties.name: ObjectColor
properties.summary: If ON::color_from_object == ColorSource, then color is the object's display color.

properties.name: ObjectDecoration
properties.summary: Used to indicate an object has a decoration (like an arrowhead on a curve)

properties.name: ObjectId
properties.summary: Every object has a Guid (globally unique identifier, also known as UUID, or universally unique identifier). The default value is Guid.Empty. When an object is added to a model, the value is checked. If the value is Guid.Empty, a new Guid is created. If the value is not null but it is already used by another object in the model, a new Guid is created. If the value is not Guid.Empty and it is not used by another object in the model, then that value persists. When an object is updated, by a move for example, the value of ObjectId persists. This value is the same as the one returned by object.Id.

properties.name: OCSMappingChannelId
properties.summary: The mapping channel id to use when calling MappingChannel to retrieve the OCS mapping if there is one.

properties.name: PlotColor
properties.summary: If plot_color_from_object == PlotColorSource, then PlotColor is the object's plotting color.

properties.name: PlotColorSource
properties.summary: The color used to plot an object on paper is specified in one of three ways. If PlotColorSource is ON::plot_color_from_layer, then the object's layer ON_Layer::PlotColor() is used. If PlotColorSource is ON::plot_color_from_object, then value of PlotColor() is used.

properties.name: PlotWeight
properties.summary: Plot weight in millimeters. =0.0 means use the default width <0.0 means don't plot (visible for screen display, but does not show on plot)

properties.name: ReceivesShadows
properties.summary: Gets or sets an object's receives shadows property, or whether or not an object receives shadows from other objects.

properties.name: RenderMaterial
properties.summary: Sets the render material for the object - higher level function for setting MaterialSource and MaterialIndex

properties.name: SectionAttributesSource
properties.summary: Where section attributes are read from

properties.name: Space
properties.summary: Starting with V4, objects can be in either model space or page space. If an object is in page space, then ViewportId is not nil and identifies the page it is on.

properties.name: Url
properties.summary: Objects may have an URL. There are no restrictions on what value this URL may have. As an example, if the object came from a commercial part library, the URL might point to the definition of that part.

properties.name: UserData
properties.summary: List of custom information that is attached to this class.

properties.name: UserDictionary
properties.summary: Dictionary of custom information attached to this class. The dictionary is actually user data provided as an easy to use shareable set of information.

properties.name: ViewportId
properties.summary: If ViewportId is nil, the object is active in all viewports. If ViewportId is not nil, then this object is only active in a specific view. This field is primarily used to assign page space objects to a specific page, but it can also be used to restrict model space to a specific view.

properties.name: Visible
properties.summary: Gets or sets an object's visibility.

properties.name: WireDensity
properties.summary: When a surface object is displayed in wireframe, this controls how many isoparametric wires are used. value number of isoparametric wires -1 boundary wires (off) 0 boundary and knot wires 1 boundary and knot wires and, if there are no interior knots, a single interior wire. N>=2 boundary and knot wires and (N+1) interior wires.
